name: Workday AI Screening in Enterprise Hiring
description: Production use of AI-assisted candidate screening workflows in enterprise hiring pipelines using Workday recruiting tooling.

deployment_context.deployment_setting: production candidate screening and ranking support

sources.title: EEOC Files First-Ever Lawsuit Against AI Hiring Software Developer Workday
Workday must face AI hiring bias lawsuit, US judge rules

transparency_explainability.claim: Regulatory filings emphasize opacity concerns for applicant-facing decision explanations.

fairness_nondiscrimination.claim: Federal court proceedings on discrimination allegations indicate elevated fairness risk in deployment context.

safety_robustness.claim: Production-grade platform controls and enterprise operations indicate moderate robustness.

privacy_data_governance.claim: Enterprise HR systems include formal data governance and retention controls.

human_agency_oversight.claim: Recruiter workflows are decision-support oriented with humans retaining final selection authority.

accountability.claim: Active legal and regulatory accountability channels are present for contested deployment outcomes.

assumptions: Scores synthesize publicly available product, regulatory, and legal evidence into the MEDF scale.
This case evaluates governance characteristics of deployed hiring automation workflows rather than proprietary model internals.
The evidence set is intended for reproducible pre-deployment governance comparison.
